content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

Verified the amendment's claims against the actual codebase. All three assertions are confirmed: (1) OpenRouter model IDs are passed through codex_new_session_args/codex_resume_args (lines 985, 991), (2) those helpers unconditionally strip -fast suffixes and inject service_tier="fast" via codex_model_reasoning_effort_and_fast (lines 1319-1335) with zero backend gating, and (3) tests for -fast handling only exist for Codex paths (lines 943-1202), with no OpenRouter regression coverage. This is a real coupling defect where Codex-specific conventions are applied to a different provider's model namespace.

## Votes

- `fr-3-9c8192c4`: Accept
  The bug is confirmed and real. The codex_model_reasoning_effort_and_fast helper (line 1319) strips -fast suffixes unconditionally, and both codex_new_session_args (line 1356) and codex_resume_args (line 1389) call it without any backend discrimination. Since build_command routes OpenRouter through these same helpers (lines 985, 991), any OpenRouter model whose legitimate ID ends in -fast would be silently truncated and have service_tier="fast" injected — potentially 404ing or targeting the wrong model. This is not theoretical: the code paths are shared and exercised in production. The remediation (gate -fast parsing to BackendFamily::Codex and add an OpenRouter regression test) is straightforward and proportionate to the medium severity.
